text:
Once upon a time in a small, sleepy village nestled among rolling hills, there was a peculiar flower garden. Every morning under the watchful gaze of sleepy owls, tiny, pink blooms would unfurl like delicate pink feathers. The garden was known far and wide for its enchanted flowers that sang sweet songs when nobody was looking.

The most remarkable flower was Old Man Willow. It shimmered like aged oak leaves and hummed ancient secrets. Every year, children would come to gather its petals, hoping to hear its dulcet lullaby.

One windy autumn day, a young girl named Daisy decided to visit the Willow. As she bent down to collect some petals, she felt a peculiar weight pull her down. The Willow grumbled softly. Daisy looked up to see its branches stretching toward her. Before she could think, they embraced her securely.

"Poor child," breathed the Willow. "You're too energetic around those pretty flowers."

Daisy felt strange but laughed it off, until...
